name: Europe
description: European countries, landmarks, and continental facts

questions.q: What is the smallest country in Europe by area?

source.notes: ~0.44 km² (110 acres). Entirely enclosed within Rome.

questions.q: Which country is the northernmost in mainland Europe?

source.notes: Norway's North Cape reaches 71° North. (Iceland is an island, not mainland).

questions.q: Which European country has Portugal as its only land neighbor?

source.notes: Iberian Peninsula. The two countries share a 1,200+ km border.

questions.q: The Arc de Triomphe is located in which European city?

source.notes: Commissioned by Napoleon in 1806. At the end of the Champs-Élysées.

questions.q: Which German state is known for Oktoberfest, Bavarian cuisine, and the Alps?

source.notes: Capital: Munich. Bavaria is the largest German state by area.

questions.q: The Flamenco dance and music tradition originates from which country?

source.notes: Specifically Andalusia. Recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage.

questions.q: Oslo is the capital of which country?

source.notes: Awards the Nobel Peace Prize annually on December 10.

questions.q: Which European country is home to the Boboli Gardens and the Uffizi Gallery?

source.notes: Both in Florence. Boboli Gardens behind Pitti Palace; Uffizi houses Renaissance art.

questions.q: How many countries are currently members of the European Union (as of 2024)?

source.notes: 28 before Brexit (UK left Jan 2020). Croatia was the last to join (2013).

questions.q: Which European country is an island nation in the North Atlantic?

source.notes: All three are European island nations. Iceland is closest to the Arctic; Malta and Cyprus in Mediterranean.

questions.q: Which country's name appears on the passport but is NOT in continental Europe?

source.notes: Greenland is part of Denmark politically but geographically in North America.

questions.q: Which river flows through both London and Oxford in England?

source.notes: 215 miles long. Flows east to the North Sea through southern England.

questions.q: The Eiffel Tower was originally built as the entrance arch to what event?

source.notes: 1889 Exposition Universelle, marking 100 years since the French Revolution.

questions.q: Which European capital is split by the Bosporus strait and sits on two continents?

source.notes: Only major city straddling two continents (Europe and Asia). Not Turkey's capital (Ankara is).

questions.q: Which mountain range forms a natural border between France and Spain?

source.notes: ~491 km long. Principality of Andorra sits within them.

questions.q: Which sea lies to the north of Germany, the Netherlands, and Denmark?

source.notes: Connects to the Atlantic Ocean. Major fishing and oil drilling region.

questions.q: Which country has borders with Germany, Poland, Austria, and Slovakia?

source.notes: Czech Republic. Part of former Czechoslovakia until 1993 'Velvet Divorce.'

questions.q: Which European country invented modern-form liberal democracy, parliamentary government, and common law?

source.notes: Magna Carta (1215), parliamentary supremacy, common law heritage. Westminster system model.

questions.q: The 'Autobahn' is the famous highway system of which European country?

source.notes: Parts have no speed limit. ~13,000 km of motorway network.

questions.q: Which European country is divided into 20 regions, including Lombardy, Tuscany, and Sicily?

source.notes: Italy's 20 regions each have significant autonomy. 5 have special status.
